passage: An agricultural study compared organic and conventional farming methods across 40 paired farm plots with similar soils, climate, and crop types over eight years. Organic plots yielded approximately 19% less per acre on average than conventional plots, but they required 45% less energy input, used no synthetic pesticides, and maintained higher soil organic matter after eight years. During two drought years in the study period, the yield gap narrowed significantly: organic plots yielded only 9% less than conventional plots in those years.
question: A farmer argues that organic farming becomes more competitive with conventional farming under drought conditions. Which data best supports this argument?

choices.A: During the two drought years, the organic-conventional yield gap narrowed from 19% to only 9%.
choices.B: Organic plots required 45% less energy input than conventional plots.
choices.C: Organic plots maintained higher soil organic matter after eight years.
choices.D: The study compared 40 paired farm plots over eight years.

explanation: A directly compares the yield gap under normal conditions (19%) versus drought conditions (9%), showing the gap narrows under drought—i.e., organic farming becomes more competitive. B and C show other advantages of organic farming (energy, soil health) but do not address drought conditions specifically. D describes study design—methodology.
